mappings.requirement_text: R-AUTH-001: Only the contract owner MUST be able to call withdraw().

locations.evidence: withdraw() has onlyOwner modifier which restricts access to the contract owner via require(msg.sender == owner).
onlyOwner modifier implements the ownership check with require(msg.sender == owner, 'Not owner').

mappings.requirement_text: R-AUTH-002: The contract owner MUST be able to add and remove addresses from the whitelist.

locations.evidence: addToWhitelist() is restricted to owner via onlyOwner modifier and sets whitelist[addr] = true.
removeFromWhitelist() is restricted to owner via onlyOwner modifier and sets whitelist[addr] = false.

mappings.requirement_text: R-AUTH-003: Only whitelisted addresses or the owner MUST be able to call deposit().

locations.evidence: deposit() exists but is missing any whitelist or owner check — any address can currently call it, violating the access restriction.

mappings.requirement_text: R-DEP-001: deposit() MUST accept ETH and credit the sender's balance.

locations.evidence: deposit() is payable and credits the sender's balance via balances[msg.sender] += msg.value.
State variable balances (mapping(address => uint256)) stores per-address deposit balances.

mappings.requirement_text: R-DEP-002: deposit() MUST revert if msg.value is 0.

locations.evidence: deposit() contains require(msg.value > 0, 'Zero deposit') which reverts on zero-value deposits.

mappings.requirement_text: R-DEP-003: A Deposited(address, uint256) event MUST be emitted on successful deposit.

locations.evidence: deposit() emits Deposited(msg.sender, msg.value) on successful execution.
Event Deposited(address indexed sender, uint256 amount) is declared at line 10.

mappings.requirement_text: R-WDR-001: withdraw(uint256 amount) MUST transfer the specified amount to the owner.

locations.evidence: withdraw() transfers ETH to msg.sender (the owner, enforced by onlyOwner) via .call{value: amount}.

mappings.requirement_text: R-WDR-002: withdraw() MUST revert if amount exceeds the contract balance.

locations.evidence: withdraw() contains require(amount <= address(this).balance, 'Insufficient balance') which reverts when amount exceeds contract balance.

mappings.requirement_text: R-WDR-003: A Withdrawn(address, uint256) event MUST be emitted on successful withdrawal.

locations.evidence: withdraw() emits Withdrawn(msg.sender, amount) during execution.
Event Withdrawn(address indexed to, uint256 amount) is declared at line 11.

mappings.requirement_text: R-WDR-004: Withdrawal SHOULD follow the checks-effects-interactions pattern.

locations.evidence: withdraw() performs external call via .call{value: amount} at line 35 before any state update — the checks-effects-interactions pattern is violated.

mappings.requirement_text: R-EMR-001: emergencyPause() MUST halt all deposits and withdrawals.

locations.evidence: emergencyPause() sets paused = true, and both deposit() and withdraw() enforce require(!paused) to halt operations.
deposit() contains require(!paused, 'Paused') which enforces the halt when paused is true.
withdraw() contains require(!paused, 'Paused') which enforces the halt when paused is true.

mappings.requirement_text: R-EMR-002: Only the owner MUST be able to call emergencyPause() and emergencyUnpause().

locations.evidence: emergencyPause() has onlyOwner modifier restricting access to the contract owner.
emergencyUnpause() is missing the onlyOwner modifier — any address can call it, violating the owner-only access restriction.

mappings.requirement_text: withdraw() parameter 'amount' must be of type uint256

locations.evidence: withdraw() declares parameter amount of type uint256 matching the specification.

mappings.requirement_text: addToWhitelist() parameter 'addr' must be of type address

locations.evidence: addToWhitelist() declares parameter addr of type address matching the specification.

mappings.requirement_text: removeFromWhitelist() parameter 'addr' must be of type address

locations.evidence: removeFromWhitelist() declares parameter addr of type address matching the specification.
